name: DIPY (Diffusion MRI Processing)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); DIPY developers / Nikhil Pappu

performance.notes: NumPy/Cython-based; tractography is CPU-intensive; large DWI datasets

agent_readiness.notes: Diffusion MRI (dMRI) processing and tractography. import dipy. from dipy.io.image import load_nifti. data, affine = load_nifti('dwi.nii.gz'). Tensor model: from dipy.reconst.dti import TensorModel; tenmodel = TensorModel(gtab); tenfit = tenmodel.fit(data, mask). FA = tenfit.fa. MD = tenfit.md. Tractography: LocalTracking, EuDX. Fiber bundles. Streamlines. Tractometry. Registration: SyNRegistration. CLI: dipy_fit_dti, dipy_track. Integrates with nibabel.

gotchas: Domain-specific — only for diffusion MRI; steep learning curve
Requires understanding of diffusion tensor imaging (DTI), b-values, b-vectors
Tractography results depend heavily on algorithm parameters
No GPU support for most algorithms — tractography is slow
No type annotations — poor IDE support
best_when: Diffusion tensor imaging (DTI/DWI) preprocessing and modeling
White matter tractography and fiber bundle analysis
summary: DIPY is the standard Python library for diffusion MRI processing and tractography. load_nifti('dwi.nii.gz'). TensorModel for DTI (FA, MD, eigenvalues). LocalTracking/EuDX for tractography. Fiber bundle analysis and tractometry. Registration. BSD-3. Best for white matter tractography, DTI modeling, and dMRI preprocessing. Requires neuroscience domain knowledge. No GPU support.
